title: acetosella strictula
side_effects: Acetosella strictula can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to liver damage and kidney failure in severe cases. Additionally, skin contact with the plant's sap may cause irritation, redness, and blistering, while inhaling its volatile compounds can trigger respiratory issues like asthma attacks. Improper handling and exposure to Acetosella strictula can also cause allergic reactions and increase the risk of infections.
